summary.intent: Address two Round 4 Codex review findings on PR #696 (audit-agnostic-navigation-components) — a subtle stale-capture bug in the SyncProps disabled_keys computation and a stale spec code snippet from the R3 fix.
summary.outcome: Both fixes implemented, tested (187 tree_view tests passing, clippy clean, spec validate + snippet-compile pass, xci-fast all 10 steps green), committed, and pushed to the worktree branch.

repo: The SyncProps arm captures `props.items` at the top of the arm before the `items_changed` decision; any disabled_keys computed there are stale when a ChildrenLoaded lazy-splice has already mutated `ctx.items` — the canonical source of truth post-load is `ctx.items`, not `props.items`.

Round-N Codex fixes can introduce subtle interactions: R2 (preserve lazy items) + R3 (recompute disabled_keys) composed to create a new staleness window only visible on an unrelated SyncProps echo after a load.

Spec code snippets under `spec/components/navigation/tree-view.md` are compiled and validated via `cargo test -p xtask --test spec_corpus_compile_snippets` and `cargo xtask spec validate`; stale pseudo-code in `rust,no_check` blocks can silently mislead adapter implementors.
xci-fast (`cargo xci --profile fast`) is the comprehensive local CI gate; targeted gates (lib tests, clippy, spec validate) are run first for fast feedback before the full xci pass.

code.finding: disabled_keys must be computed INSIDE the SyncProps closure after the items_changed decision, reading from ctx.items (which may contain lazy-spliced children from ChildrenLoaded). Computing it before from props.items produces a stale set that drops disabled children added post-load, allowing SelectNode to admit them.

New TDD test `lazy_loaded_disabled_child_stays_unselectable_across_unrelated_sync_props` covers the specific regression: disabled child loaded via ChildrenLoaded must remain unselectable after a subsequent unrelated SyncProps echo.

§6 RenameCommit snippet was synced to R3 logic: reject only when renaming_key is None (not on key mismatch), always emit Effect::Rename, gate the renaming_key clear on key match. The old snippet would silently drop a user's edit on a retarget handoff.

workflow: Multi-round Codex review (R1–R4+) is iterative: each round's fixes can introduce interaction bugs caught only in the next round; budget for compound rounds when fixing state-machine logic.
GitHub GraphQL reviewThreads API filtered by `isResolved==false` and author matching `codex` is the reliable way to enumerate outstanding Codex threads between rounds.
Defensive re-check via reactions API (`gh api repos/.../issues/696/reactions`) confirms no stale +1 from prior rounds when tallying new Codex feedback.

friction: R4-T2 was a non-obvious second-order interaction: the bug only manifests when (1) ChildrenLoaded runs, (2) a subsequent unrelated SyncProps echo preserves ctx.items, and (3) that echo overwrites disabled_keys with the stale pre-load set — invisible to any single-step test.

Codex round-n fixes compound: each round introduces potential for new interaction bugs with previous rounds, requiring re-running the full xci gate after every set of fixes rather than accumulating changes.
open_items: Post-push: reply on R4-T1 and R4-T2 GitHub threads, mark both resolved, and re-trigger Codex for a potential Round 5 review.
Monitor whether R4 fixes surface any further SyncProps/lazy-load interaction edge cases in subsequent Codex rounds.
